title: Generating getters/setters for ports
body:
Given some Verilog code like
```
module mymodule(input bit a, output bit b);
    // do something with a to get b
endmodule
```
After `verilator -CFLAGS -fPIC mymodule.sv`, I'm able to get a `Vmymodule` C++ class that I can interact with, i.e. `dut->a = 0`. I'm trying to interact with this from Rust, and `autocxx` does not support setting member variables on non-POD classes ([reference](https://google.github.io/autocxx/cpp_types.html#pod-and-non-pod)), so it would be useful if Verilator could generate a `set_a`/`get_b` method, since I could call that from Rust. 

Right now I'm hacking around it by redefining the macros `VL_IN8` and `VL_OUT8` and etc, but it would be great to not have to do this. Ideally, this would be something I could enable with a flag when I run `verilator`.

I'm happy to try and implement this myself if it is something that would be a good idea to include into Verilator.
